---
bibliography = "assets/library.bib"
number-sections = true
toc = true
---

```{=html}
<div style="text-align: center; margin-bottom: 1.5rem;">
  <img class="logo-light" src="/assets/Calepin.svg" alt="Calepin logo" style="max-width: 280px;">
  <img class="logo-dark" src="/assets/Calepin_dark.svg" alt="Calepin logo" style="max-width: 280px;">
  <p style="font-size: 1.1rem; color: var(--c-text-muted); margin-top: 0.5rem;">A notebook, website, and documentation generator written in Rust.</p>
</div>
```

{% include "source_tip" %}

*Calepin* is a fast rendering engine for Quarto-compatible notebooks, written in Rust. It turns `.qmd` files into HTML, LaTeX, Typst, or Markdown, executes R and Python code chunks, and builds websites and books. The name comes from the French word for a small pocket notebook.

::: {.callout-tip}
Click the `</>` button in the top right to view the `.qmd` source code side by side with the rendered output on any page of this site.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
*Calepin* is alpha software. Please report bugs and feature requests at [github.com/vincentarelbundock/calepin](https://github.com/vincentarelbundock/calepin).
:::

Fast:

- Written in Rust.
- Sub-millisecond rendering for typical notebooks.
- Render many documents in parallel with the built-in batch mode.
- Code chunk outputs are cached, so code only runs once (or when it changes).
- Live preview with instant reload as you edit.

Extensible:

- Write filters, custom functions, and postprocessors in Python, R, shell, or anything that reads JSON.
- Override templates with plain HTML, LaTeX, or Typst files. No programming required.

Templates everywhere:

- Every element is rendered through a small Jinja template that you can override by dropping a file in `_calepin/partials/`.
- Document elements: figures, tables, code blocks, code output, callouts, theorems, proofs, equations, citations, footnotes, table of contents, abstracts, author blocks, affiliations.
- Page layout: the full page wrapper, preamble, title block, date, subtitle, keywords, appendix, license, copyright, funding.
- Website chrome: navbar, sidebar, footer, breadcrumbs, search, theme toggle, page navigation, source viewer.
- One CSS file controls all styling. One TOML file controls all configuration.

LLM-friendly:

- Every piece of output is governed by a simple Jinja template and plain CSS.
- HTML output is classless and minimal. No framework, no JavaScript build step.
- Simple, plain CSS. No preprocessor, no utility classes.
- The entire stack uses well-understood technologies: Markdown, HTML, Jinja, TOML.
- LLMs already know these formats inside out, so you can describe what you want and let your LLM build or customize your website, templates, and styles directly.
